aetherion: The lifeblood of galactic civilisation. Without it, ships don't jump, trade dies, and empires stall. Fresh deposits are rare enough that they start wars. Alliances form and fracture over a single promising survey report. Everyone wants it. Nobody has enough. What makes it stranger: Aetherion doesn't just power Foldstream entry — it seems to anchor the corridors themselves. Systems rich in it sit at natural crossroads. Control the crystal, control the routes.
foldstream: A higher-dimensional manifold where spacetime folds into currents. Ships that can decouple from their inertial frame — using Aetherion — slip into these currents and cross interstellar distances without relativistic travel. It behaves like an ocean: some routes are fast, well-charted, and heavily trafficked. Others are turbulent, shorter, and dangerous. Controlling a Foldstream node means controlling everything that moves between stars. Factions don't just fight over Aetherion mines. They fight over which mines sit closest to the corridors.
foldstream-perception: Inside the Foldstream, normal senses stop being reliable. Pilots report impossible colours at the edge of vision, tones that resonate in bone rather than ear, and memories that aren't theirs. Instruments pick up phase fluctuations that occasionally resemble structured language. Whether that's noise, something alien, or the Foldstream itself is a question nobody has answered cleanly.
temporal-drift: Time doesn't run clean in the Foldstream. Most ships re-emerge synchronised to realspace. Most. Anomalies are rare but documented — ships arriving days before they left, or crews stepping out to find a century gone. Aetherion navigation matrices reduce the risk significantly. They don't eliminate it. Every long-haul pilot knows someone with a story.
dead-currents: Some Foldstream corridors lead nowhere charted — out into the void between galaxies, or into regions where physics stops behaving predictably. Navigators call them Dead Currents and leave them blank on the charts. Smugglers use them anyway. So do certain cults. Neither group talks much about what they've seen on the other side.
somnium: A world that shouldn't exist by any conventional model — it phases in and out of real space, dragging its two moons with it. When it's present, it shows up on every scanner. When it's not, there's no trace it was ever there. Fresh Aetherion deposits have been reported on the surface. Nobody who went looking has come back with a simple answer.
